project.name: ar-tata-surya
project.currentFocus: Android/Vercel WebAR camera cover fitting, tilted GLB presentation, GLB scale normalization, and planet texture recovery
durableFacts: AR experience depends on A-Frame and AR.js webcam video layering and readiness checks.
GLB solar system and focus planet sizing is normalized with an A-Frame auto-model-scale component using THREE.Box3 target sizes.
Some planet GLBs use legacy material data, so focus mode includes runtime texture recovery from public/textures when GLB maps are missing.

Mobile camera visibility is protected by leaving AR.js webcam source dimensions unmanaged while AR.js display dimensions and viewport-aware cover fitting expand the video/canvas layers across the Android visual viewport.

lastAchievements: Applied normalized sizing to solar_system.glb and focus planet GLBs with per-planet multipliers.
Compacted mobile focus overlays and preserved AR camera layering.
Added viewport-width fitting for AR camera/canvas surfaces to avoid right-side cropping on mobile Chrome.
Added material recovery for focus planet GLBs and rotated solar_system.glb toward a horizontal Sketchfab-like preview orientation.
Removed forced AR.js source/display dimensions and changed camera/canvas fitting to container-relative 100% layers.
Added camera-not-ready error handling so the app does not hide loading into a black AR canvas.
Added AR-to-landing cleanup for loading state, hash state, scroll position, and idempotent control bindings.
Passed Android visual viewport dimensions into AR.js displayWidth/displayHeight during scene creation.
Added Android viewport-aware video cover fitting so AR.js camera streams expand beyond mismatched source aspect ratios.
Tilted solar-system and focus GLB roots so the model presentation is less edge-on/vertical on marker scans.
Build passed with npm.cmd run build.
pendingIssues: Verify normalized GLB sizing on Vercel HTTPS with a physical Hiro marker.
Check Mars, Jupiter, Saturn, and solar_system orientation on Infinix Note 40 portrait.
Verify camera feed on deployed HTTPS after committing/pushing because the screenshot came from the hosted URL.
nextBestActions: Deploy the local patch to Vercel through the connected GitHub main branch or a manual Vercel deploy.
Use ?debug=true to confirm viewport, AR Vid, Vid CSS, bounding box, recovered material logs, and AR video readiness on deployed HTTPS.
Tune SOLAR_SYSTEM_VIEW_ROTATION, FOCUS_PLANET_VIEW_ROTATION, DEVICE_TARGET_SIZE_MAP, or per-planet focusTargetSizeMultiplier if a model still feels too vertical, large, or small.
validationState: npm.cmd run build: pass after Android AR.js display sizing, camera cover-fit, and GLB tilt patch
npm.cmd run validate --if-present: pass/no configured script output
